claims.statement: Beyond HSR, the mergers are also conditioned on (i) the expiration of applicable waiting periods and authorization or consent from a certain foreign regulator under its antitrust laws and (ii) authorization or consent of applicable governmental authorities for certain of UniFirst's permits.
claims.quote: Other Approvals The mergers are also conditioned upon (i) the expiration of applicable waiting periods and authorization or consent from a certain foreign regulator under its antitrust laws and (ii) the authorization or consent of the applicable governmental authorities in respect of certain of UniFirst’s permits.

claims.statement: Completion of the mergers requires approval of the merger proposal by UniFirst shareholders.
claims.quote: Approval of the merger proposal by UniFirst shareholders is required for completion of the mergers.
claims.locator: Questions and Answers about the Special Meeting and the Mergers

claims.statement: Closing is also conditioned on the Cintas shares to be issued in the mergers being approved for listing on NASDAQ: neither party is required to consummate the mergers if such shares are not approved for listing.
claims.quote: The merger agreement provides that neither Cintas nor UniFirst will be required to consummate the mergers if such shares are not approved for listing on the NASDAQ, subject to official notice of issuance.
claims.locator: The Merger Agreement—Conditions to Completion of the Mergers (p. 92)

claims.statement: The merger agreement contains a Company-side Material Adverse Effect bring-down: it represents that since August 30, 2025 through the date of the agreement there has not been any event that has had, or would reasonably be expected to have, individually or in the aggregate, a Material Adverse Effect on UniFirst (the Company).
claims.quote: (c) Since August 30, 2025 through the date of this Agreement, there has not been any event, circumstance, occurrence, effect, fact, development or change that has had, or would reasonably be expected to have, individually or in the aggregate, a Material Adverse Effect on the Company.
claims.locator: Annex A, Agreement and Plan of Merger, Art. III Representations and Warranties of the Company (Absence of Certain Changes)

claims.statement: The merger agreement also contains a Parent-side Material Adverse Effect bring-down: it represents that since May 31, 2025 through the date of the agreement there has not been any event that has had, or would reasonably be expected to have, individually or in the aggregate, a Material Adverse Effect on Cintas (Parent).
claims.quote: (b) Since May 31, 2025 through the date of this Agreement, there has not been any event, circumstance, occurrence, effect, fact, development or change that has had, or would reasonably be expected to have, individually or in the aggregate, a Material Adverse Effect on Parent.
claims.locator: Annex A, Agreement and Plan of Merger, Art. IV Representations and Warranties of Parent (Absence of Certain Changes)

claims.statement: The merger agreement restricts UniFirst's ability to solicit, seek, facilitate, discuss or enter into an agreement with respect to a UniFirst acquisition proposal (a no-shop covenant).
claims.quote: The merger agreement contains certain provisions that restrict UniFirst’s ability to solicit, seek, facilitate, discuss or enter into an agreement with respect to a UniFirst acquisition proposal.

claims.statement: The merger agreement provides for a target-side termination fee of $213.3 million payable by UniFirst to Cintas and a reverse termination fee of $350 million payable by Cintas to UniFirst, each in specified termination circumstances.
claims.quote: The Merger Agreement provides for the payment by UniFirst to Cintas of a termination fee of $213.3 million if the Merger Agreement is terminated in specified circumstances, and for payment by Cintas to UniFirst of a termination fee of $350 million if the Merger Agreement is terminated in specified circumstances.
claims.locator: Item 1.01 Entry into a Material Definitive Agreement — Termination Fees

claims.statement: The merger agreement is enforceable by specific performance: each party agreed it will not oppose the granting of an injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief on the basis that another party has an adequate remedy at law or that specific performance is not an appropriate remedy.
claims.quote: Each of the parties agrees that it will not oppose the granting of an injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief on the basis that any other party has an adequate remedy at law or that any award of specific performance is not an appropriate remedy for any reason at law or in equity.
claims.locator: Annex A, Agreement and Plan of Merger, Section 8.10 Specific Performance

claims.statement: Cintas reaffirmed that it would agree to a reverse termination fee of no more than $350.0 million, and that it would require two four-month extensions at the end of a 10-month termination date to the extent needed to clear antitrust approvals.
claims.quote: Following the above discussions, Cintas reaffirmed its position that it would agree to a reverse termination fee of no more than $350.0 million, and that it would require two four-months extensions at the end of a 10 months termination date to the extent needed to clear antitrust approvals.

claims.statement: Shareholder-vote risk is low but on a related-party footing: the Croatti family's >two-thirds voting control plus a voting-and-support agreement made approval effectively certain — yet it is the same family the activist accused of entrenchment that delivered the vote, so minority holders had little practical say in price discovery.
